narratives.evidence_quote: amerikans are the greatest pigs on the planet…and that is in the most derogitive term you can imagine.. absolute pigs
narratives.reasoning: This quote directly and harshly criticizes and dehumanizes the people of a Western nation (America), aligning with the narrative of discrediting the West through extreme negative portrayal.

narratives.narrative_name: URW: Negative Consequences for the West
narratives.evidence_quote: lets get on with a full nuke war…wipe us all out …at least it (for most) will be over quickly
narratives.reasoning: This statement highlights and seemingly advocates for a catastrophic adverse outcome (nuclear war) for Western countries, predicting their destruction as a consequence of current policies.

narratives.evidence_quote: Will they resort to nuclear weapons to even out the war?
narratives.reasoning: This question, posed in the introductory text, evokes fear and anxiety about the potential for nuclear escalation, which is a core element of amplifying war-related fears.

narratives.evidence_quote: Sadly the western media is framing the war as if Russia is losing which only prolongs negotiations and causes more death.
narratives.reasoning: This statement directly questions the reliability and motives of Western media, accusing it of harmful misrepresentation, which undermines confidence in media sources.

In the context of the interview where Ritter is presented as explaining 'what is really going on' counter to Western media, praising him as 'talking the truth' implicitly praises the Russian perspective he is associated with conveying.
This phrase from the title is a direct prediction/assumption about the potential result of the conflict, which is the definition of speculating on war outcomes.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Discrediting the West, Diplomacy: The West is overreacting
subnarratives.evidence_quote: amerika always blew any comment about RUSSIA or at later stages CHINA way out of proportion.. and you are still doing
subnarratives.reasoning: This quote directly accuses the West (specifically America) of consistently and disproportionately exaggerating threats from Russia and China, which aligns with the definition of presenting the West's reaction as disproportionate.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Discrediting the West, Diplomacy: The West does not care about Ukraine, only about its interests
subnarratives.evidence_quote: Sadly the western media is framing the war as if Russia is losing which only prolongs negotiations and causes more death. The question is, why the desperation to keep the war going?
subnarratives.reasoning: This quote implies that the Western media's framing (and by extension, Western political interests) is deliberately prolonging the war in Ukraine, causing more death, rather than genuinely caring about a peaceful resolution for Ukraine's sake.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Discrediting the West, Diplomacy: Diplomacy does/will not work
subnarratives.evidence_quote: Scott Ritter ..good topics ..talking the truth..yes but where is what he says getting us …seems nowhere
subnarratives.reasoning: This statement expresses futility about the impact of truthful discussion (a form of diplomatic or informative effort), suggesting that such talk leads 'nowhere', thereby discrediting the potential of diplomatic or reasoned discourse to resolve the situation.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Discrediting the West, Diplomacy: Other
subnarratives.evidence_quote: I do not think an amerikan life is worth 1/2 as much as an African (or any other life outside the west) ..they are trash ..not worth the effort ..get to a point of “kill them all” either because they do not care and they want us all dead ..or because they just sit back and “DO NOT CARE”
subnarratives.reasoning: This quote supports the parent narrative of discrediting the West by portraying Western (specifically American) populations as morally bankrupt, uncaring, and deserving of destruction. It is a virulent attack on Western values and people that does not fit the more specific diplomatic subnarratives but clearly aims to discredit the West fundamentally.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Negative Consequences for the West: Sanctions imposed by Western countries will backfire
subnarratives.evidence_quote: for the west it’s always been the soviet (RUSSIAN) that has ALWAYS been the boogie man..when in fact they were SELDOM a threat.. amerika always blew any comment about RUSSIA or at later stages CHINA way out of proportion.. and you are still doing
subnarratives.reasoning: This quote argues that Western (specifically American) demonization and overreaction to Russia and China is a persistent, misguided policy. It implies that this aggressive posture, which includes sanctions and confrontation, is based on a false premise ('seldom a threat') and is therefore a self-inflicted negative consequence for the West, aligning with the idea that such policies backfire by creating unnecessary conflict.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Negative Consequences for the West: Other
subnarratives.evidence_quote: lets get on with a full nuke war…wipe us all out …at least it (for most) will be over quickly and no more of month after month of promises from Trump or threats from the east….POOF..it’s all over …thanks mostly to un-elected bureaucrats in all “western countries” they are proving the worst of the worst of humanity
subnarratives.reasoning: This quote presents a catastrophic, nihilistic outcome for the West (a full nuclear war leading to annihilation) as a direct consequence of the actions of 'un-elected bureaucrats in all "western countries"'. It supports the parent narrative of negative consequences for the West but does not fit the specific definitions of sanctions backfiring or refugee flows; it instead argues for a broader, existential negative consequence stemming from Western governance and policy.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Amplifying war-related fears: There is a real possibility that nuclear weapons will be employed
subnarratives.evidence_quote: lets get on with a full nuke war…wipe us all out …at least it (for most) will be over quickly
subnarratives.reasoning: This quote directly evokes fear and anxiety about the use of nuclear weapons, framing a full nuclear war as a conceivable, if not desired, outcome to end the conflict.

subnarratives.evidence_quote: The question is, why the desperation to keep the war going? Will they resort to nuclear weapons to even out the war?
subnarratives.reasoning: This text explicitly raises the specter of nuclear weapon use as a potential act of desperation, which amplifies war-related fears. While it relates to nuclear weapons, the primary thrust is questioning motives and framing the threat as a sign of desperation, which is not fully captured by the specific 'possibility of use' subnarrative alone. The overall tone of despair and inevitability of catastrophic conflict supports the parent narrative in a way that extends beyond the specific definitions.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Distrust towards Media: Western media is an instrument of propaganda
subnarratives.evidence_quote: Another must listen to interview with information that you are missing from the corporation propaganda western media
subnarratives.reasoning: This quote directly labels Western media as 'corporation propaganda,' framing it as a tool that withholds or distorts information, which aligns with the definition of discrediting Western media institutions as instruments of propaganda.

subnarratives.evidence_quote: Sadly the western media is framing the war as if Russia is losing which only prolongs negotiations and causes more death.
subnarratives.reasoning: This statement supports the parent narrative of distrust towards media by accusing Western media of a specific, harmful misrepresentation (framing Russia as losing) that has real-world consequences (prolonging war). It discredits Western media but does not fit the more specific definitions of being an 'instrument of propaganda' or targeting Ukrainian media; it is a distinct argument about misleading framing.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Praise of Russia: Russia is a guarantor of peace and prosperity
subnarratives.evidence_quote: Former Intelligence Officer and Expert on Weapons of Mass Destruction, Scott Ritter, joins the program to give us a better understanding of what is really going on in Ukraine. Sadly the western media is framing the war as if Russia is losing which only prolongs negotiations and causes more death.
subnarratives.reasoning: The quote frames the Western media's portrayal of Russian losses as false and harmful, implying that accepting Russia's position (presumably winning) would lead to quicker peace, thus portraying Russia as a party whose success is conducive to ending conflict and suffering.

subnarratives.evidence_quote: for the west it’s always been the soviet (RUSSIAN) that has ALWAYS been the boogie man..when in fact they were SELDOM a threat.. amerika always blew any comment about RUSSIA or at later stages CHINA way out of proportion..
subnarratives.reasoning: This statement directly praises Russia by countering the Western 'boogie man' narrative, arguing Russia has been historically misrepresented and was seldom a real threat. This supports the parent narrative of praising Russia but does not fit the more specific definitions about military, Putin, peace guarantor, international support, or invasion support.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Speculating war outcomes: Ukrainian army is collapsing
subnarratives.reasoning: The title directly states that Russia is winning, which implies the opposing force, the Ukrainian army, is losing or collapsing in its ability to resist effectively.

subnarratives.evidence_quote: the western media is framing the war as if Russia is losing which only prolongs negotiations and causes more death. The question is, why the desperation to keep the war going? Will they resort to nuclear weapons to even out the war?
subnarratives.reasoning: This quote speculates on the motivations and potential future actions (resorting to nuclear weapons) of one side to 'even out the war', which is a speculative statement about a potential war outcome that does not fit the more specific definitions about army collapse or loss of territory.
